query: Who is the current Secretary-General of the United Nations?
final_answer:
The current Secretary-General of the United Nations is **António Guterres** from Portugal. He has held this position since January 1, 2017, and is the ninth person to serve as UN Secretary-General.

**Sources:**
- https://www.un.org/sg/en/content/sg/biography
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Secretary-General_of_the_United_Nations

query: What year was the LIGO observatory's first detection of gravitational waves announced?
final_answer:
The LIGO observatory's first detection of gravitational waves was announced in **2016**. Specifically, the announcement was made on February 11, 2016, at a press conference. The actual detection had occurred on September 14, 2015, when LIGO observed gravitational waves from two merging black holes located about 1.3 billion light-years away. This historic discovery confirmed a major prediction of Einstein's theory of general relativity.

**Sources:**
- https://www.ligo.caltech.edu/LA/video/ligo20160211v11
- https://www.virgo-gw.eu/science/gw-universe/the-first-detections/
- https://www.ligo.caltech.edu/news/ligo20160914
- https://www.jpl.nasa.gov/edu/resources/teachable-moment/gravitational-waves-detected-for-the-first-time/

query: What is the capital of Mongolia?
final_answer:
The capital of Mongolia is **Ulaanbaatar** (also spelled Ulan Bator). It is the largest city in Mongolia with a population of over 1.6 million people and is notably the coldest capital city in the world by average temperature.

**Sources:**
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ulaanbaatar
- https://www.travelbuddies.info/capital-of-mongolia/
- https://www.ebsco.com/research-starters/geography-and-cartography/ulan-bator-mongolia

Ulaanbaatar is the capital of Mongolia. Between the 1980s and the 2010s, about 20 percent of Mongolia's population has moved to Ulaanbaatar; by 2018, two-thirds of the city's population were living in ger communities.
